canonical_question: How do I implement and use a Trie (prefix tree)?
aliases: trie data structure insert search delete
trie vs hash map for string search
constraints: Memory usage grows with alphabet size: each node may hold up to |SIGMA| child pointers (26 for lowercase, 128 for ASCII, 1M+ for Unicode)
Tries are not cache-friendly due to pointer chasing; for latency-critical paths, consider array-based or DAFSA alternatives
Delete operations must not orphan subtrees: always check whether a node still has children before removing it
For Unicode strings, use hash-map children (not fixed arrays) to avoid allocating millions of slots per node

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need exact-match lookup only, no prefix queries
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Use a hash set or hash map for O(1) average-case lookups

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Strings are very long (>1 KB) and few share prefixes
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Use a hash map or suffix array; trie depth equals string length

inputs_needed.question: What is the primary use case for the trie?
What character set will keys use?
